chosen_action.title: Stop hold-window reuse from masking the current guarded lane

why_this_action.summary: During an active measurement hold, run.py could reuse a stale measurement_hold_follow_through artifact before re-evaluating the current lane. That masked the truthful guarded lane state even when the selector now pointed at distribution_architecture_guard_pause instead of measurement_hold, which risked another fake-idle hold loop.
shared_findings_used: agents/marketing/logs/distribution_lane_latest.json: latest lane had already advanced past plain measurement_hold during the current review window
drafts/marketing_execution_board_latest.md: the board explicitly said no truthful do-now packet remained in the current review window
agents/marketing/logs/marketing_workflow_audit_latest.json: Codeberg movement is still flat, so runtime truthfulness matters more than placeholder hold reuse
agents/marketing/run.py: hold-window branch reused measurement_hold follow-through without first honoring the current selector lane

result.summary: Patched the hold-window path to re-evaluate the current distribution lane before reusing any old measurement-hold follow-through, and to reuse guarded architecture executions the same way the normal path does. Added a regression test and reran the live marketing loop, which now records distribution_architecture_guard_pause instead of collapsing back to measurement_hold.

replacement_condition: If another hold-window run still reports measurement_hold while the selector truth has already advanced to a guarded or repair lane, escalate into a broader run.py hold-path contract repair.
